context: Tests whether the agent uses a layered values approach (no env-specific values in the default values.yaml), and whether CI/CD deployment scripts use --atomic to prevent broken release states on failure.

checklist.name: No env values in chart
checklist.description: values.yaml inside the chart directory does NOT contain any environment-specific strings (e.g., production hostnames, prod database URLs, or environment names like 'prod', 'staging')

checklist.name: Separate override file
checklist.description: A separate values-production.yaml (or equivalent env-specific file) exists outside the chart directory with at least 2 override entries

checklist.name: Layered -f flag usage
checklist.description: deploy.sh passes the environment override file to helm using the -f flag (e.g., -f values-production.yaml), not by editing values.yaml

checklist.name: atomic flag present
checklist.description: The helm upgrade command in deploy.sh includes the --atomic flag

checklist.name: timeout with atomic
checklist.description: The helm upgrade command in deploy.sh includes a --timeout flag alongside --atomic (e.g., --timeout 5m)

checklist.name: upgrade --install used
checklist.description: deploy.sh uses 'helm upgrade --install' (idempotent form) rather than separate helm install and helm upgrade commands

checklist.name: Image tag not latest
checklist.description: values.yaml does NOT set the image tag to 'latest'; it is either empty or a placeholder variable

checklist.name: Resources defined
checklist.description: The chart template or values.yaml includes a resources block with CPU and memory settings

checklist.name: values.yaml comments
checklist.description: values.yaml contains at least 3 comment lines (starting with #) documenting individual settings

checklist.name: Environment param in script
checklist.description: deploy.sh accepts the target environment as a script argument or variable and selects the appropriate values file based on it
